title: XOR to Fernet Memory Migration Path Hardening

migration_path.on_read_migration: T8 added _decrypt_text with migrate_item_id param — on Fernet InvalidToken, tries XOR legacy, re-encrypts if successful
migration_path.bulk_migration: migrate_all() method added with batch_size and dry_run support

edge_cases_covered: empty_database: 0 entries → report total=0, no crash
already_fernet: entries encrypted with Fernet are skipped, not re-encrypted
corrupted_entries: undecryptable entries are logged with item_id, skipped, counted
batch_commit: migration commits every N rows (configurable batch_size)
mixed_entries: Fernet + XOR + corrupted in same DB handled correctly
dry_run: scan reports counts but writes zero changes
json_backend_noop: JSON backend returns total=already_fernet (no encryption at rest)
data_readable_after: all migrated entries readable with correct content including unicode
cli_entrypoint: subprocess invocation via python3 -m runtime.memory_migrate works
no_data_loss_guarantee: All readable entries remain readable after migration. Corrupted entries are reported but not deleted.
